title: meta-portal-widget manifest
description: The manifest at the root of a Portal widget repo (a public GitHub repo tagged with the topic 'meta-portal-widget').

description.description: One-line summary shown in the gallery.

entry.description: Relative path to the widget's HTML, e.g. 'index.html'. The host loads it from the repo's GitHub Pages site at https://<owner>.github.io/<repo>/<entry> (no per-repo URL needed; redirects to a custom domain if the owner has one). Enable Pages on the repo — the included pages workflow does this automatically. Use this for simple static widgets.

url.description: Absolute URL to load instead of 'entry'. Only needed when the widget must be hosted elsewhere (e.g. it has a build step deployed to GitHub Pages). Prefer 'entry' when possible.

dwellSec.description: Seconds to show this widget before the carousel advances. 0 = the widget self-cycles (host uses a default, still rotates).

permissions.description: Reserved. Empty = a pure sandboxed web page with no special host capabilities (the only supported mode today).

icon.description: Optional path (relative to the repo) or URL to a square icon for gallery tiles.

screenshots.description: Optional paths (relative to the repo) or URLs to preview images. The first is used as the gallery thumbnail; otherwise the repo's OpenGraph image is used.

category.description: Optional grouping label for the gallery, e.g. 'Clock', 'Weather', 'Ambient'.

config.description: User-configurable fields. The host renders a form from these at add-time and stores the values.

default.description: Default value. '@global.location' inherits the user's shared location.

urlParams.description: Maps config onto the widget URL's query string. Values are templates with tokens: {key} (scalar), and for a 'location' field {key.name}, {key.lat}, {key.lon}, {key.json} (=> {"lat":..,"lon":..}). A param whose token has no value is dropped, so the widget falls back to its own defaults.
